name: Consistent unification of general relativity and quantum field theory
summary: General relativity and quantum field theory are individually validated to extraordinary precision in their separate regimes, but become mutually incompatible at the Planck scale (Lambda_Pl ~ 10^19 GeV) where curvature and quantum fluctuations both matter. Perturbative quantisation of GR is non-renormalisable (two-loop counterterm shown by Goroff-Sagnotti 1986), and the resulting effective field theory breaks down well before the Planck scale. A theory of everything must supply a UV-complete framework that reproduces both GR and the Standard Model in their respective regimes, while resolving singularities (Big Bang, black hole interior) and the black-hole information paradox. This is the headline open problem the term 'theory of everything' was coined for.

characteristic_numbers.expected: scale where quantum and gravitational effects both unsuppressed
characteristic_numbers.note: Below ~ M_Pl, GR-as-EFT is consistent; above it, perturbative quantisation gives uncontrolled counterterms. The boundary is operational, not derived.
Goroff-Sagnotti 1986: the (1/epsilon) pole in the two-loop effective action contains a Riemann^3 counterterm with this coefficient, proving GR is perturbatively non-renormalisable.

principles_stressed.note: Black-hole evaporation in semi-classical gravity appears to violate unitarity; resolving this requires a quantum theory of gravity. Hawking's 1976 calculation; Page-Mathur firewall and island-formula resolutions in the 2010s.

Perturbative QG quantises fluctuations around a fixed background, but the background itself should be dynamical in any complete theory. Different programs (LQG, CDT, causal sets) take this as foundational; string theory historically did not but has moved toward it via holography.
Holographic and entropic-gravity arguments suggest QG cannot be local in the bulk-QFT sense; the holographic principle constrains bulk d.o.f. by boundary area.

history: DeWitt 1967 first attempted canonical quantisation of GR. 't Hooft-Veltman 1974 showed GR + scalar is non-renormalisable at one loop on-shell; Goroff-Sagnotti 1986 showed pure GR diverges at two loops. Donoghue 1994 reframed GR as an effective field theory below Lambda_Pl. String theory (1968-2000s), loop quantum gravity (1986-), causal dynamical triangulations (1998-), asymptotic safety (Weinberg 1979, Reuter 1998), causal set theory (1987-), and Horava-Lifshitz (2009) are the main programs of active research. The black hole information paradox (Hawking 1976) and the Page curve (Page 1993; island formula 2019-2020) sharpened the unitarity demand.

references.citation: DeWitt, 'Quantum theory of gravity. I. The canonical theory', Phys. Rev. 160, 1113 (1967)
Goroff and Sagnotti, 'The ultraviolet behavior of Einstein gravity', Nucl. Phys. B 266, 709 (1986)
Donoghue, 'General relativity as an effective field theory', PRD 50, 3874 (1994)
Kiefer, 'Quantum Gravity', International Series of Monographs on Physics 155, Oxford (3rd ed. 2012)
Rovelli and Vidotto, 'Covariant Loop Quantum Gravity', Cambridge (2014)
